outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Live updates: Houthi missile hits tanker in Red Sea; Israel strikes Lebanon — 14 killed
outlets.summary: CNN leads with the IDF strike package on 7 southern Lebanon towns killing 14, alongside the Houthi anti-ship missile strike on Andromeda Star. Frames concurrent escalations on multiple fronts as ceasefire architecture buckling. Goldman Q4 Brent forecast at $90 underscores prolonged disruption.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Trump is facing an increasingly patient Iran as oil prices spike and peace talks stall
outlets.summary: NBC frames Iran as outlasting US pressure — analytical lens suggesting Tehran's patience strategy may be working. Oil spike to $107.97 underscores economic costs of stalemate. Iran's new proposal positions Tehran as reasonable party.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump warns Iran: 4 days left — President dismisses War Powers vote as 'nonsense'
outlets.summary: Fox News leads with Trump's Truth Social ultimatum and Fox Sunday Briefing interview ('They can come to us, or they can call us'). Frames May 1 War Powers vote as toothless and the 3-carrier CENTCOM deployment as evidence of US dominance ahead of Hegseth's '38 ships redirected' update.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Israel secretly deployed Iron Dome to UAE — first foreign deployment
outlets.summary: Right-leaning outlets emphasize Israel-UAE military cooperation as vindication of Abraham Accords legacy. Iron Dome success story. Bennett-Lapid alliance seen as political threat to Netanyahu.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill 14, including women and children; evacuation orders for 7 towns
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with the IDF strike package on 7 southern Lebanon towns and the Lebanese Health Ministry casualty figures (14 killed including 2 women and 2 children, 37 wounded). Frames Araghchi's Putin meeting as a deliberate pivot to Russian mediation after Trump cancelled the US envoy trip.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: King Charles arrives in Washington for state visit as Iran war dominates agenda
outlets.summary: BBC frames King Charles visit through prism of UK-US relations strained by Iran war. 29 MPs called for cancellation. Trump-Starmer Hormuz call signals UK-France initiative on freedom of navigation.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Liveblog April 27: IDF hits 7 southern Lebanon towns; Netanyahu: Hezbollah is 'dismantling the ceasefire'
outlets.summary: Times of Israel leads with the IDF strike package on 7 towns north of the Litani and Netanyahu's video address defending the strikes as imminent self-defense against active rocket assembly. Frames the Apr 26 Hezbollah rocket fire and drone launch as the trigger for the broader response.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Report: Israel sent Iron Dome battery, troops to help defend UAE during Iran war
outlets.summary: Haaretz emphasizes the unprecedented Israel-Gulf military cooperation. Analysis frames this as new era of Middle East security architecture. Concern about provoking further Iranian escalation against Gulf states.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Goldman Sachs admits Iran has changed global oil reality — Pezeshkian: 'forecasts are psychological warfare'
outlets.summary: Press TV frames Goldman's $90 Q4 Brent forecast as Western admission that Iranian Hormuz strategy has restructured the oil market. Highlights Pezeshkian's 'psychological warfare' line and Khamenei's office statement that Hormuz remains 'closed by sovereign right.'

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Iran proposes Hormuz reopening — nuclear program talks to follow in separate track
outlets.summary: Iranian media frames the new proposal as Iran taking the initiative for peace while US remains bellicose. Blockade framed as the primary obstacle. Commercial flight resumption highlighted as victory narrative.
